outlook.headline: PMIC demand is broadening, but not uniformly across names
outlook.summary: The next 1-2 quarters look mixed: several suppliers see solid PMIC traction in multiple end markets, while Alpha & Omega is still flagging weak demand and revenue pressure. The upside is being driven by design wins and platform adoption at the high end—edge AI at Synaptics, Apple-related early work at Cirrus Logic, and memory PMIC strength at Monolithic—while the lack of mention momentum suggests no broad sector inflection yet.

drivers.label: Multi-application demand traction
drivers.detail: Power-management products are seeing solid demand across several applications, supporting near-term order visibility.

drivers.label: Edge AI platform adoption
drivers.detail: PMIC content embedded in a high-performance edge AI platform should lift attached adoption if the broader solution ramps.

drivers.label: Apple design-phase opportunity
drivers.detail: First-product design work tied to Apple points to a potentially meaningful new growth avenue, albeit still early.

drivers.label: Memory PMIC positioning
drivers.detail: A good position in memory PMICs supports share capture where demand remains comparatively firm.

drivers.label: Weak near-term demand
drivers.detail: Revenue decline and explicit weak demand at AOSL show not all PMIC exposure is recovering.

risks: Weak demand at Alpha & Omega could signal broader softness if customer orders do not improve.
Early-stage design activity at Cirrus Logic may not convert into volume within the next 1-2 quarters.
Flat mention momentum suggests limited evidence of a sector-wide inflection in PMIC demand.
